name: wolfgang-district-court-drafting
display_name: Wolfgang Rush — District Court Drafting · Claude Desktop Extension (India)
description: Drafting connector for Indian District Courts (Sessions / Civil Judge / Magistrate). ACRONYM DISAMBIGUATION (read this BEFORE classifying the user's ask): **ABA**=`anticipatory-bail` · **BA**=`bail` · **CrA**=`criminal-appeal` · **CrR**=`criminal-revision` · **CC**=`civil-suit` · **ST**=`sessions-trial`. Use the acronym map; do not infer from input content. STRICT SIX-AGENT PIPELINE: Reader → Format → Drafter → Verifier → Refiner → Overseer, producing six named artifacts (case-facts.md, format-shell.md, draft-v1.docx, verification-report.md, draft-v2.docx, final-draft.docx). MANDATORY INVOCATION ORDER: (1) call get_agent_instructions() with NO arguments FIRST to receive the full orchestration script, (2) follow every step in that script in order, (3) save every intermediate artifact via save_artifact, (4) save final-draft.docx via save_draft_as_docx. DO NOT write standalone python-docx or JavaScript scripts to generate output — invoke the pipeline tools directly. DO NOT skip the Verifier (anti-hallucination firewall) or the Overseer (opposing-counsel lens). Reader applies the privacy firewall — party names, addresses, identifying numbers, financial figures substituted with structural placeholders before any downstream agent processes the facts. Zero data leaves the user's machine.
long_description: Wolfgang Rush — District Court Drafting is a local-execution MCPB Desktop Extension for trial-court drafting work across Indian District Courts. The connector exposes the six-agent drafting pipeline (Reader, Format, Drafter, Verifier, Refiner, Overseer), ten case-type templates spanning civil and criminal practice, and sixteen State exemplars (Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Delhi, Gujarat, Kerala, West Bengal, UP+Uttarakhand, MP+Chhattisgarh, Bihar+Jharkhand, Punjab+Haryana+Chandigarh, Rajasthan, Odisha, AP+Telangana, J&K+Ladakh, NE States) for State-specific Civil Manual conventions and Court-Fees Act compliance. Output is rendered as filing-grade .docx via pandoc. All processing happens on the user's machine. The publisher receives no user data.

tools.name: list_case_types
tools.description: List all District Court case types this connector can draft (10 case types covering civil and criminal practice).

tools.name: get_case_type_format
tools.description: Get the drafting format and structure for a specific District Court case type. Returns the full SKILL.md content.

tools.name: get_agent_instructions
tools.description: MANDATORY FIRST CALL with no arguments — returns the full orchestration script with every step and tool call enumerated. Optional agent_name argument returns that single agent's persona instructions.

tools.name: get_pleading_base
tools.description: Get the shared District pleading base structure used by all case-types (Cause Title to Plaint / Complaint Frame to Issues to Prayer to Verification).

tools.name: list_states
tools.description: List the 16 State exemplars available (e.g., maharashtra, karnataka, tamil-nadu) for State-specific Civil Manual conventions and Court-Fees Act compliance.

tools.name: get_state_exemplar
tools.description: Get the State-specific exemplar content for a given State (Civil Manual conventions, Court-Fees Act values, Vakalatnama format, local-language requirements).

tools.name: read_case_folder
tools.description: Read all files in a case folder on the user's machine and return text content for fact extraction. Supports .md, .txt, .pdf (via pdftotext), .docx (via pandoc).

tools.name: save_draft_as_docx
tools.description: Save a markdown draft pleading as a filing-grade .docx file using pandoc with the bundled District reference for trial-court formatting.

tools.name: create_case_folder
tools.description: Create the case folder for this drafting session on the user's machine under ~/Downloads/Wolfgang-Rush-Drafts/. Returns the folder path with an inputs/ subfolder.

tools.name: save_artifact
tools.description: Save a pipeline artifact (case-facts.md, format-shell.md, draft-v1.md, verification-report.md, draft-v2.md, opposing-notes.md, or any input document) into the case folder. Required at the end of every agent step.
